skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem: Data set A consists of 17 values, listed in ascending order: 30, 30, 31, 31, 32, 32, 33, 34, 34, 35, 35, 36, 36, 37, 37, 38, 38. Data set B is created by adding 72 to each of the values in data set A. Which of the following statements correctly compares data set B to data set A?

answerOptions.content: The median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A, and the range of data set B is equal to the range of data set A.
The median of data set B is equal to the median of data set A, and the range of data set B is greater than the range of data set A.
The median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A, and the range of data set B is greater than the range of data set A.
The median of data set B is greater than the median of data set A, and the range of data set B is equal to the range of data set A.

rationale: Adding the same constant to every value increases the median by that same constant (the median of B is 34 + 72 = 106, greater than A's median of 34), but does not change the range, since both the maximum and the minimum shift by the same amount (A: range = 38−30 = 8; B: range = 110−102 = 8).
